resources.string: Speak a transcript, or type one and tap Send to drive the dispatch pipeline end-to-end. Server URL and permissions live in the Configurator (overflow menu).
Lets Zwangli type dispatch responses into whatever app you are currently in. Grant this permission so voice commands can write text into your focused field.
Accessibility service is OFF — tap below to enable, then return.
⚠ no editable field focused — text was not typed
Keeps Zwangli reachable from the status bar so you can dictate without opening the app first.
Speech recognition unavailable on this device
Microphone permission denied — using text input instead
Tap "Test connection" to ping the server and list its enabled verbs.
Types dispatch responses into whichever app is focused. Required for the dictate-anywhere flow.
Captures speech for the on-device recognizer.
Shows the persistent dictation notification with the Dictate / Stop actions.
Foreground service that keeps Zwangli reachable from the status bar so you can dictate without opening the app first.
Granted at install. Lets the Clock app create alarms and timers from voice commands.
By default, voice searches go through Android\'s system search engine. To override (e.g., DuckDuckGo, Kagi, a self-hosted instance), enter a URL template below using {query} as the placeholder.
e.g. https://duckduckgo.com/?q={query} • Leave blank to use system default.
Template must contain {query} and start with http(s)://
No browser or search app could handle the request.

Phrases the server listens for at the start of a transcript. \"zwingli alarm 7am wake up\" works because \"zwingli\" is here. Tap the × on a chip to remove it; type a new phrase below and tap Add to add one.
Lowercase letters, optionally with single spaces (e.g. \"computer\" or \"hey assistant\").
Server unreachable — cannot manage activation phrases until the connection works.
Bearer token rejected — check the token above.
No activation phrases configured. Add at least one or the server can\'t dispatch any voice command.
Cannot remove the last phrase — the dispatcher needs at least one.
Lowercase letters with single-space separators only (2–40 chars).
Remove \"%1$s\"? Voice commands starting with that word will no longer be dispatched.
